message:
fix(storage): use factory for backend-aware storage creation (bd-m2jr)

Multiple code paths were bypassing the storage factory and creating
SQLite databases directly, ignoring sync.mode=dolt-native configuration.
This caused SQLite/JSONL files to be created inappropriately when Dolt
connections failed transiently.

Changes:
- routed.go: Use GetRoutedStorageWithOpener with factory.NewFromConfig
- ship, swarm, move, refile, ready, graph: Replace sqlite.New with factory
- show.go: Use factory + simplify by using Storage interface methods
- doctor/*: Use factory for backend-aware storage creation
- CheckCompactionCandidates: Add backend check + CompactableStorage assertion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,13 +5,14 @@ import (
 	"encoding/json"
 	"fmt"
 	"os"
+	"path/filepath"
 	"sort"
 	"strings"
 
 	"github.com/spf13/cobra"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/rpc"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/storage"
-	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/storage/sqlite"
+	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/storage/factory"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/types"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/ui"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/utils"
@@ -75,9 +76,10 @@ Status icons: ○ open  ◐ in_progress  ● blocked  ✓ closed  ❄ deferred`,
 		}
 
 		// If daemon is running but doesn't support this command, use direct storage
+		// Use factory to respect backend configuration (bd-m2jr: SQLite fallback fix)
 		if daemonClient != nil && store == nil {
 			var err error
-			store, err = sqlite.New(ctx, dbPath)
+			store, err = factory.NewFromConfig(ctx, filepath.Dir(dbPath))
 			if err != nil {
 				fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "Error: failed to open database: %v\n", err)
 				os.Exit(1)

-	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/storage/sqlite"
+	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/storage/factory"
 	"github.com/steveyegge/beads/internal/types"

 )
@@ -98,8 +97,8 @@ Examples:

-		targetDBPath := filepath.Join(targetBeadsDir, "beads.db")
-		targetStore, err := sqlite.New(ctx, targetDBPath)
+		// Use factory to respect backend configuration (bd-m2jr: SQLite fallback fix)
+		targetStore, err := factory.NewFromConfig(ctx, targetBeadsDir)
 		if err != nil {
 			FatalError("failed to open target rig database: %v", err)
 		}

 	Run: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
 		// Use global jsonOutput set by PersistentPreRun (respects config.yaml + env vars)
 		// If daemon is running but doesn't support this command, use direct storage
+		// Use factory to respect backend configuration (bd-m2jr: SQLite fallback fix)
 		ctx := rootCtx
 		if daemonClient != nil && store == nil {
 			var err error
-			store, err = sqlite.New(ctx, dbPath)
+			store, err = factory.NewFromConfig(ctx, filepath.Dir(dbPath))

 				os.Exit(1)

@@ -53,8 +54,10 @@ func runShip(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {
 	var err error
 
 	// Ship requires direct store access for label operations
+	// Use factory to respect backend configuration (bd-m2jr: SQLite fallback fix)
 	if daemonClient != nil && store == nil {
-		store, err = sqlite.New(ctx, dbPath)
+		beadsDir := filepath.Dir(dbPath)
+		store, err = factory.NewFromConfig(ctx, beadsDir)
 		if err != nil {

 			os.Exit(1)
